# maestro-fetch

> Universal data acquisition toolkit. Fetch structured data from any URL.

maestro-fetch is a Python library and MCP server that fetches data from any URL:
web pages, APIs, PDFs, Excel/CSV, cloud storage (Dropbox, Google Drive),
and video/audio (YouTube). It auto-detects the source type and returns
structured content as markdown, tables (pandas DataFrames), and metadata.

## Quick Start (MCP Server for AI Agents)

Install: pip install maestro-fetch[mcp]

Add to Claude Code .mcp.json:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "maestro-fetch": {
      "command": "maestro-fetch-mcp"
    }
  }
}

## MCP Tools

### fetch_url
Fetch data from any single URL. Auto-detects source type.
Parameters:
- url (string, required): The URL to fetch
- output_format (string, optional): "markdown" | "json" | "csv". Default: "markdown"
- provider (string, optional): "anthropic" | "openai". Only needed with schema extraction.
Returns: { url, source_type, content, table_count, metadata }

### batch_fetch_urls
Fetch multiple URLs concurrently.
Parameters:
- urls (list of strings, required): URLs to fetch
- output_format (string, optional): "markdown" | "json" | "csv"
- concurrency (integer, optional): Max parallel requests. Default: 5
Returns: list of { url, source_type, content, table_count }

### detect_url_type
Classify a URL into source type without downloading.
Parameters:
- url (string, required): URL to classify
Returns: { url, source_type } where source_type is "web" | "doc" | "cloud" | "media"

## Python SDK

from maestro_fetch import fetch, batch_fetch

# Basic fetch (no API key needed)
result = await fetch("https://example.com/data")
result.content      # markdown text
result.tables       # list[pd.DataFrame]
result.source_type  # "web" | "doc" | "cloud" | "media"
result.metadata     # dict

# With custom headers
result = await fetch(url, headers={"Authorization": "Bearer token"})

# Batch fetch
results = await batch_fetch(urls, concurrency=10)

# LLM extraction (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY)
result = await fetch(url, schema={"field": str}, provider="anthropic")

## URL Type Detection

*.pdf -> doc (PDF parsing via Docling/pdfplumber)
*.xlsx, *.xls, *.csv -> doc (spreadsheet parsing via openpyxl/pandas)
dropbox.com/*, drive.google.com/* -> cloud (resolve share link + download)
youtube.com/watch*, youtu.be/* -> media (yt-dlp + Whisper transcription)
Everything else -> web (headless browser via Crawl4AI)
